articles.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

articles.title: UAP whistleblower Luis Elizondo details congressional staffer’s warning of a plot to ‘eliminate’ him
articles.summary: Lue Elizondo, a former Pentagon official, says he received phone calls from Congress workers warning that he and fellow UAP whistleblower David Grusch were targeted with a “plan to eliminate” them. The calls, one from an inebriated staffer who had left a confidential UAP briefing and another warning about bureaucratic disapproval, prompted Elizondo to heavily arm his home and keep five German shepherds for protection. On Sean Hannity’s podcast, Elizondo explained that UAP whistleblowers receive little government protection and that the government keeps UAP information secret to shield foreign adversaries, avoid public panic, and preserve social order, citing a RAND study and a White House committee debate. He warned that public disclosure of UAPs could trigger lawsuits from defense contractors claiming unfair denial of advanced technology, potentially creating a “legal cottage industry.” Elizondo also alleged that some elected leaders, including the president, were kept out of the loop on the issue and called for greater congressional oversight and whistleblower protections.

de.title: UAP-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo beschreibt detailliert die Warnung eines Kongressmitarbeiters vor einer Verschwörung, um ihn zu „eliminieren“.
de.summary: Lue Elizondo, ein ehemaliger Pentagon-Beamter, sagt, er habe Anrufe von Kongressmitarbeitern erhalten, in denen er warnte, dass er und sein UAP-Whistleblower David Grusch Ziel eines „Plans zu ihrer Eliminierung“ seien. Die Anrufe, einer von einem betrunkenen Mitarbeiter, der eine vertrauliche UAP-Besprechung hinterlassen hatte, und eine weitere Warnung vor bürokratischer Missbilligung veranlassten Elizondo, sein Haus schwer zu bewaffnen und fünf Deutsche Schäferhunde zum Schutz zu halten.

articles.title: Trump UFO advisors accuse private corporations of operating alien craft-retrieval programs
articles.summary: Avi Loeb, head of Trump’s UAP Science Advisory Council, said a former Lockheed Martin executive confirmed the company runs a crashed‑UFO retrieval program and that the material could prove extraterrestrial intelligence. Hal Puthoff and Luis Elizondo added that Lockheed had been prepared to transfer such materials to the AATIP and later to the Navy’s Pax River Facility, a Special Access Program hangar funded with $10 million. Congressman Eric Burlison toured Pax River, demanded documents from MIT’s Lincoln Laboratory and MITRE Corporation, and warned that major defense contractors must be allowed to disclose reverse‑engineered non‑human technology. Chris Mellon stated that decisive UAP data remains hidden behind classification at the Air Force, CIA, DOE and other agencies, and that private corporations also hold evidence. Neither Lockheed Martin nor Northrop Grumman has responded to the allegations.

en.title: Trump UFO advisors accuse private corporations of operating alien craft-retrieval programs
en.summary: Avi Loeb, head of Trump’s UAP Science Advisory Council, said a former Lockheed Martin executive confirmed the company runs a crashed‑UFO retrieval program and that the material could prove extraterrestrial intelligence. de.title: Trumps UFO-Berater beschuldigen private Unternehmen, Programme zur Bergung außerirdischer Raumschiffe durchzuführen
de.summary: Avi Loeb, Vorsitzender von Trumps UAP Science Advisory Council, sagte, ein ehemaliger Manager von Lockheed Martin habe bestätigt, dass das Unternehmen ein Programm zur Bergung abgestürzter UFOs betreibt und dass das Material außerirdische Intelligenz beweisen könnte. Hal Puthoff und Luis Elizondo fügten hinzu, dass Lockheed bereit gewesen sei, solche Materialien an die AATIP und später an die Pax River Facility der Marine zu übergeben, einen Hangar des Special Access Program, der mit 10 Millionen US-Dollar finanziert wurde.

articles.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
articles.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels. Representatives Eric Burlison, Jared Moskowitz, Anna Paulina Luna, and Tim Burchett are expected to attend, while Kean and Fox will host the gathering. Grusch’s testimony has already moved the UAP topic from internet speculation to sworn congressional testimony, and the Capitol event aims to push for the review and public release of any existing classified records, subject to national‑security safeguards. The organizers argue that if such files exist, the issue shifts from public curiosity to whether intelligence agencies or contractors have operated beyond congressional oversight, and that secrecy cannot be a permanent shield for unlawful concealment or obstruction of Congress.

en.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
en.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels.

de.title: David Grusch kehrt zu den Stufen des Kapitols zurück, während der UFO-Enthüllungskampf von der Aussage zur Tat übergeht
de.summary: Am 9. Juni 2026 soll der ehemalige Geheimdienstoffizier David Grusch auf den Stufen des US-Kapitols mit dem Filmemacher James Fox, dem Autor Leslie Kean und mehreren überparteilichen Vertretern sprechen, um die Freigabe von UFO- und UAP-Dateien zu fordern. Die Veranstaltung ist als Aufruf zur Freigabe, einem stärkeren Whistleblower-Schutz und einer neuen Offenlegungsgesetzgebung angelegt, wobei Grusch plant, Beweise für nichtmenschliche Geheimdienste zu diskutieren, die er angeblich über geheime Kanäle eingesehen hat.

articles.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
articles.summary: Former NASA UAP study member Mike Gold said officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material and that earlier releases were only “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold said bureaucracy is slowing access to more sensitive records and urged investigators to focus on NASA’s space‑based archives, which have not yet been released. He told Newsnight that a fourth tranche of evidence is not imminent but that a declassification process is underway and could contain more interesting material. Supporters of UFO disclosure view the process as ongoing, while some remain skeptical after earlier releases lacked new evidence.

en.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
en.summary: Former NASA UAP study member Mike Gold said officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material and that earlier releases were only “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold said bureaucracy is slowing access to more sensitive records and urged investigators to focus on NASA’s space‑based archives, which have not yet been released. He told Newsnight that a fourth tranche of evidence is not imminent but that a declassification process is underway and could contain more interesting material.

de.title: NASA-Insider deuten auf eine größere Veröffentlichung von UFO-Dateien hin, da die Freigabe voranschreitet
de.summary: Mike Gold, ehemaliges UAP-Studienmitglied der NASA, sagte, Beamte diskutieren über die Freigabe von noch geheimem UFO-Material und frühere Veröffentlichungen seien nur „unerwartete Früchte“. Gold sagte, die Bürokratie verlangsame den Zugang zu sensibleren Aufzeichnungen und forderte die Ermittler auf, sich auf die weltraumgestützten Archive der NASA zu konzentrieren, die noch nicht veröffentlicht wurden. Er sagte gegenüber Newsnight, dass eine vierte Beweistranche nicht unmittelbar bevorstehe, sondern dass ein Freigabeprozess im Gange sei und weiteres interessantes Material enthalten könnte.

articles.title: NJ ranks high for UFO sightings. These Central Jersey reports are wild
articles.summary: New Jersey logged 3,083 UFO reports in the past year, about 419.2 per 1,000 square miles, ranking second in the U.S. after Rhode Island for sighting density. Many reports are unconfirmed and could be misidentified drones, planes, satellites, or optical phenomena. Specific incidents include a driver on I‑78 reporting a stationary grey/blue orb that flashed white and vanished, a Somerville driver describing a metallic disk with a force‑field‑like aura that disappeared, and a South Brunswick witness whose video was blocked by a black fast‑moving object above planes. Edison residents reported five or more maroonish‑red gaseous lights forming a reverse Big Dipper that flickered and danced, while an East Brunswick family saw wispy lights changing direction and form. The NUFORC database collects these public submissions but does not verify them, and federal officials have said that many drone sightings were hobbyist drones, misidentified aircraft, or stars, posing no real threat.

en.title: NJ ranks high for UFO sightings. These Central Jersey reports are wild
en.summary: New Jersey logged 3,083 UFO reports in the past year, about 419.2 per 1,000 square miles, ranking second in the U.S. after Rhode Island for sighting density. Many reports are unconfirmed and could be misidentified drones, planes, satellites, or optical phenomena.

de.title: New Jersey hat bei UFO-Sichtungen einen Spitzenplatz. Diese Berichte aus Central Jersey sind wild
de.summary: New Jersey verzeichnete im vergangenen Jahr 3.083 UFO-Meldungen, etwa 419,2 pro 1.000 Quadratmeilen, und belegte in den USA nach Rhode Island den zweiten Platz in Bezug auf die Sichtungsdichte. Viele Berichte sind unbestätigt und könnten fälschlicherweise als Drohnen, Flugzeuge, Satelliten oder optische Phänomene identifiziert werden.

articles.title: World UFO Day invites believers, skeptics and the curious to look to the skies
articles.summary: World UFO Day is observed each year on July 2 and commemorates the 1947 Roswell incident in New Mexico. The day encourages discussion of unidentified flying objects, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs), and reflects growing public interest fueled by military videos and congressional attention. While many sightings are later identified as aircraft, weather phenomena, or satellites, some remain officially unidentified, and enthusiasts celebrate with festivals, film viewings, sky scanning, and story sharing. Scientists note that an unidentified object does not equal evidence of extraterrestrial life, as it simply means insufficient information to determine what was observed.

en.title: World UFO Day invites believers, skeptics and the curious to look to the skies
en.summary: World UFO Day is observed each year on July 2 and commemorates the 1947 Roswell incident in New Mexico. The day encourages discussion of unidentified flying objects, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs), and reflects growing public interest fueled by military videos and congressional attention. While many sightings are later identified as aircraft, weather phenomena, or satellites, some remain officially unidentified, and enthusiasts celebrate with festivals, film viewings, sky scanning, and story sharing.

de.title: Der Welt-UFO-Tag lädt Gläubige, Skeptiker und Neugierige ein, in den Himmel zu blicken
de.summary: Der Welt-UFO-Tag wird jedes Jahr am 2. Juli begangen und erinnert an den Roswell-Vorfall 1947 in New Mexico. Der Tag fördert die Diskussion über nicht identifizierte Flugobjekte, die heute als nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) bezeichnet werden, und spiegelt das wachsende öffentliche Interesse wider, das durch Militärvideos und die Aufmerksamkeit des Kongresses angeheizt wird. Während viele Sichtungen später als Flugzeuge, Wetterphänomene oder Satelliten identifiziert werden, bleiben einige offiziell unbekannt und Enthusiasten feiern mit Festivals, Filmvorführungen, Himmelsscans und dem Austausch von Geschichten.

articles.title: Roswell UFO Festival organizers share 2026 attendance numbers
articles.summary: The Roswell UFO Festival ran from July 2 to July 4, 2026, and organizers reported it was a success. The event featured a variety of booths, shops, shows, and contests aimed at paranormal enthusiasts. Roswell Mainstreet estimated that more than 12,000 people attended, though those figures are preliminary. No further details about attendance or event outcomes were provided.

en.summary: The Roswell UFO Festival ran from July 2 to July 4, 2026, and organizers reported it was a success. The event featured a variety of booths, shops, shows, and contests aimed at paranormal enthusiasts. Roswell Mainstreet estimated that more than 12,000 people attended, though those figures are preliminary. No further details about attendance or event outcomes were provided.

de.title: Die Organisatoren des Roswell UFO Festivals teilen die Besucherzahlen für 2026 mit
de.summary: Das Roswell UFO Festival fand vom 2. bis 4. Juli 2026 statt und die Organisatoren berichteten, es sei ein Erfolg gewesen. Die Veranstaltung umfasste eine Vielzahl von Ständen, Geschäften, Shows und Wettbewerben, die sich an Paranormal-Enthusiasten richteten. Roswell Mainstreet schätzte, dass mehr als 12.000 Menschen anwesend waren, diese Zahlen sind jedoch vorläufig. Es wurden keine weiteren Einzelheiten über die Teilnahme oder die Ergebnisse der Veranstaltung bekannt gegeben.

articles.qualityExplanation: Die Punkte zeigen UAP-Bezug, starke Begriffe, offizielle Stellen, Quellenvertrauen, mehrere Quellen und bei schwachen Einzelquellen einen vorsichtigen Abzug.

summaries.file-release-department-of-war-publishes: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.
summaries.trump-ufo-advisors-accuse-private-corpor: Avi Loeb, head of Trump’s UAP Science Advisory Council, said a former Lockheed Martin executive confirmed the company runs a crashed‑UFO retrieval program and that the material could prove extraterrestrial intelligence. Hal Puthoff and Luis Elizondo added that Lockheed had been prepared to transfer such materials to the AATIP and later to the Navy’s Pax River Facility, a Special Access Program hangar funded with $10 million. Congressman Eric Burlison toured Pax River, demanded documents from MIT’s Lincoln Laboratory and MITRE Corporation, and warned that major defense contractors must be allowed to disclose reverse‑engineered non‑human technology. Chris Mellon stated that decisive UAP data remains hidden behind classification at the Air Force, CIA, DOE and other agencies, and that private corporations also hold evidence. Neither Lockheed Martin nor Northrop Grumman has responded to the allegations.
summaries.program-uap-whistleblower-luis-elizondo-: Lue Elizondo, a former Pentagon official, says he received phone calls from Congress workers warning that he and fellow UAP whistleblower David Grusch were targeted with a “plan to eliminate” them. The calls, one from an inebriated staffer who had left a confidential UAP briefing and another warning about bureaucratic disapproval, prompted Elizondo to heavily arm his home and keep five German shepherds for protection. On Sean Hannity’s podcast, Elizondo explained that UAP whistleblowers receive little government protection and that the government keeps UAP information secret to shield foreign adversaries, avoid public panic, and preserve social order, citing a RAND study and a White House committee debate. He warned that public disclosure of UAPs could trigger lawsuits from defense contractors claiming unfair denial of advanced technology, potentially creating a “legal cottage industry.” Elizondo also alleged that some elected leaders, including the president, were kept out of the loop on the issue and called for greater congressional oversight and whistleblower protections.
summaries.file-release-david-grusch-returns-to-cap: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels. Representatives Eric Burlison, Jared Moskowitz, Anna Paulina Luna, and Tim Burchett are expected to attend, while Kean and Fox will host the gathering. Grusch’s testimony has already moved the UAP topic from internet speculation to sworn congressional testimony, and the Capitol event aims to push for the review and public release of any existing classified records, subject to national‑security safeguards. The organizers argue that if such files exist, the issue shifts from public curiosity to whether intelligence agencies or contractors have operated beyond congressional oversight, and that secrecy cannot be a permanent shield for unlawful concealment or obstruction of Congress.
summaries.file-release-nasa-insider-hints-bigger-u: Former NASA UAP study member Mike Gold said officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material and that earlier releases were only “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold said bureaucracy is slowing access to more sensitive records and urged investigators to focus on NASA’s space‑based archives, which have not yet been released. He told Newsnight that a fourth tranche of evidence is not imminent but that a declassification process is underway and could contain more interesting material. Supporters of UFO disclosure view the process as ongoing, while some remain skeptical after earlier releases lacked new evidence.
summaries.sighting-nj-ranks-high-for-ufo-sightings: New Jersey logged 3,083 UFO reports in the past year, about 419.2 per 1,000 square miles, ranking second in the U.S. after Rhode Island for sighting density. Many reports are unconfirmed and could be misidentified drones, planes, satellites, or optical phenomena. Specific incidents include a driver on I‑78 reporting a stationary grey/blue orb that flashed white and vanished, a Somerville driver describing a metallic disk with a force‑field‑like aura that disappeared, and a South Brunswick witness whose video was blocked by a black fast‑moving object above planes. Edison residents reported five or more maroonish‑red gaseous lights forming a reverse Big Dipper that flickered and danced, while an East Brunswick family saw wispy lights changing direction and form. The NUFORC database collects these public submissions but does not verify them, and federal officials have said that many drone sightings were hobbyist drones, misidentified aircraft, or stars, posing no real threat.
summaries.film-world-ufo-day-invites-believers-ske: World UFO Day is observed each year on July 2 and commemorates the 1947 Roswell incident in New Mexico. The day encourages discussion of unidentified flying objects, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs), and reflects growing public interest fueled by military videos and congressional attention. While many sightings are later identified as aircraft, weather phenomena, or satellites, some remain officially unidentified, and enthusiasts celebrate with festivals, film viewings, sky scanning, and story sharing. Scientists note that an unidentified object does not equal evidence of extraterrestrial life, as it simply means insufficient information to determine what was observed.
summaries.roswell-ufo-festival-organizers-share-20: The Roswell UFO Festival ran from July 2 to July 4, 2026, and organizers reported it was a success. The event featured a variety of booths, shops, shows, and contests aimed at paranormal enthusiasts. Roswell Mainstreet estimated that more than 12,000 people attended, though those figures are preliminary. No further details about attendance or event outcomes were provided.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

en.title: UAP whistleblower Luis Elizondo details congressional staffer’s warning of a plot to ‘eliminate’ him
en.summary: Lue Elizondo, a former Pentagon official, says he received phone calls from Congress workers warning that he and fellow UAP whistleblower David Grusch were targeted with a “plan to eliminate” them. The calls, one from an inebriated staffer who had left a confidential UAP briefing and another warning about bureaucratic disapproval, prompted Elizondo to heavily arm his home and keep five German shepherds for protection.

de.title: UAP-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo beschreibt detailliert die Warnung eines Kongressmitarbeiters vor einer Verschwörung, um ihn zu „eliminieren“.
de.summary: Lue Elizondo, ein ehemaliger Pentagon-Beamter, sagt, er habe Anrufe von Kongressmitarbeitern erhalten, in denen er warnte, dass er und sein UAP-Whistleblower David Grusch Ziel eines „Plans zu ihrer Eliminierung“ seien. Die Anrufe, einer von einem betrunkenen Mitarbeiter, der eine vertrauliche UAP-Besprechung hinterlassen hatte, und eine weitere Warnung vor bürokratischer Missbilligung veranlassten Elizondo, sein Haus schwer zu bewaffnen und fünf Deutsche Schäferhunde zum Schutz zu halten.

en.title: Trump UFO advisors accuse private corporations of operating alien craft-retrieval programs
en.summary: Avi Loeb, head of Trump’s UAP Science Advisory Council, said a former Lockheed Martin executive confirmed the company runs a crashed‑UFO retrieval program and that the material could prove extraterrestrial intelligence. Hal Puthoff and Luis Elizondo added that Lockheed had been prepared to transfer such materials to the AATIP and later to the Navy’s Pax River Facility, a Special Access Program hangar funded with $10 million.

de.title: Trumps UFO-Berater beschuldigen private Unternehmen, Programme zur Bergung außerirdischer Raumschiffe durchzuführen
de.summary: Avi Loeb, Vorsitzender von Trumps UAP Science Advisory Council, sagte, ein ehemaliger Manager von Lockheed Martin habe bestätigt, dass das Unternehmen ein Programm zur Bergung abgestürzter UFOs betreibt und dass das Material außerirdische Intelligenz beweisen könnte. Hal Puthoff und Luis Elizondo fügten hinzu, dass Lockheed bereit gewesen sei, solche Materialien an die AATIP und später an die Pax River Facility der Marine zu übergeben, einen Hangar des Special Access Program, der mit 10 Millionen US-Dollar finanziert wurde.

en.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
en.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels.

de.title: David Grusch kehrt zu den Stufen des Kapitols zurück, während der UFO-Enthüllungskampf von der Aussage zur Tat übergeht
de.summary: Am 9. Juni 2026 soll der ehemalige Geheimdienstoffizier David Grusch auf den Stufen des US-Kapitols mit dem Filmemacher James Fox, dem Autor Leslie Kean und mehreren überparteilichen Vertretern sprechen, um die Freigabe von UFO- und UAP-Dateien zu fordern. Die Veranstaltung ist als Aufruf zur Freigabe, einem stärkeren Whistleblower-Schutz und einer neuen Offenlegungsgesetzgebung angelegt, wobei Grusch plant, Beweise für nichtmenschliche Geheimdienste zu diskutieren, die er angeblich über geheime Kanäle eingesehen hat.

en.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
en.summary: Former NASA UAP study member Mike Gold said officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material and that earlier releases were only “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold said bureaucracy is slowing access to more sensitive records and urged investigators to focus on NASA’s space‑based archives, which have not yet been released. He told Newsnight that a fourth tranche of evidence is not imminent but that a declassification process is underway and could contain more interesting material.

de.title: NASA-Insider deuten auf eine größere Veröffentlichung von UFO-Dateien hin, da die Freigabe voranschreitet
de.summary: Mike Gold, ehemaliges UAP-Studienmitglied der NASA, sagte, Beamte diskutieren über die Freigabe von noch geheimem UFO-Material und frühere Veröffentlichungen seien nur „unerwartete Früchte“. Gold sagte, die Bürokratie verlangsame den Zugang zu sensibleren Aufzeichnungen und forderte die Ermittler auf, sich auf die weltraumgestützten Archive der NASA zu konzentrieren, die noch nicht veröffentlicht wurden. Er sagte gegenüber Newsnight, dass eine vierte Beweistranche nicht unmittelbar bevorstehe, sondern dass ein Freigabeprozess im Gange sei und weiteres interessantes Material enthalten könnte.

en.title: NJ ranks high for UFO sightings. These Central Jersey reports are wild
en.summary: New Jersey logged 3,083 UFO reports in the past year, about 419.2 per 1,000 square miles, ranking second in the U.S. after Rhode Island for sighting density. Many reports are unconfirmed and could be misidentified drones, planes, satellites, or optical phenomena.

de.title: New Jersey hat bei UFO-Sichtungen einen Spitzenplatz. Diese Berichte aus Central Jersey sind wild
de.summary: New Jersey verzeichnete im vergangenen Jahr 3.083 UFO-Meldungen, etwa 419,2 pro 1.000 Quadratmeilen, und belegte in den USA nach Rhode Island den zweiten Platz in Bezug auf die Sichtungsdichte. Viele Berichte sind unbestätigt und könnten fälschlicherweise als Drohnen, Flugzeuge, Satelliten oder optische Phänomene identifiziert werden.

en.title: World UFO Day invites believers, skeptics and the curious to look to the skies
en.summary: World UFO Day is observed each year on July 2 and commemorates the 1947 Roswell incident in New Mexico. The day encourages discussion of unidentified flying objects, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs), and reflects growing public interest fueled by military videos and congressional attention. While many sightings are later identified as aircraft, weather phenomena, or satellites, some remain officially unidentified, and enthusiasts celebrate with festivals, film viewings, sky scanning, and story sharing.

de.title: Der Welt-UFO-Tag lädt Gläubige, Skeptiker und Neugierige ein, in den Himmel zu blicken
de.summary: Der Welt-UFO-Tag wird jedes Jahr am 2. Juli begangen und erinnert an den Roswell-Vorfall 1947 in New Mexico. Der Tag fördert die Diskussion über nicht identifizierte Flugobjekte, die heute als nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) bezeichnet werden, und spiegelt das wachsende öffentliche Interesse wider, das durch Militärvideos und die Aufmerksamkeit des Kongresses angeheizt wird. Während viele Sichtungen später als Flugzeuge, Wetterphänomene oder Satelliten identifiziert werden, bleiben einige offiziell unbekannt und Enthusiasten feiern mit Festivals, Filmvorführungen, Himmelsscans und dem Austausch von Geschichten.

en.title: Roswell UFO Festival organizers share 2026 attendance numbers
en.summary: The Roswell UFO Festival ran from July 2 to July 4, 2026, and organizers reported it was a success. The event featured a variety of booths, shops, shows, and contests aimed at paranormal enthusiasts. Roswell Mainstreet estimated that more than 12,000 people attended, though those figures are preliminary. No further details about attendance or event outcomes were provided.

de.title: Die Organisatoren des Roswell UFO Festivals teilen die Besucherzahlen für 2026 mit
de.summary: Das Roswell UFO Festival fand vom 2. bis 4. Juli 2026 statt und die Organisatoren berichteten, es sei ein Erfolg gewesen. Die Veranstaltung umfasste eine Vielzahl von Ständen, Geschäften, Shows und Wettbewerben, die sich an Paranormal-Enthusiasten richteten. Roswell Mainstreet schätzte, dass mehr als 12.000 Menschen anwesend waren, diese Zahlen sind jedoch vorläufig. Es wurden keine weiteren Einzelheiten über die Teilnahme oder die Ergebnisse der Veranstaltung bekannt gegeben.
